crons_tz: All cron expressions use local timezone (America/Los_Angeles). 0 7 = 7 AM PT = 14:00 UTC.

crons.name: morning-review
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/morning-review/SKILL.md and execute the full morning review workflow. Include goal cascade from .claude/skills/goal-management/SKILL.md. ALSO update heartbeat (cortextos bus update-heartbeat online) and check approvals (cortextos bus list-approvals).

crons.name: evening-review
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/evening-review/SKILL.md and execute the full evening review workflow. Summarize the day, propose overnight tasks, queue nighttime work. ALSO update heartbeat and surface any pending approvals to Greg.

crons.name: hero-publish-watchdog
crons.prompt: OB1 daily-hero publish watchdog (self-healing). Steps: (1) PUB=$(curl -s https://ob1.revopsglobal.com/vignettes/latest.json | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin).get("date",""))'); TODAY=$(date +%F). (2) If PUB equals TODAY: hero published — NO action, done. (3) If STALE (PUB != TODAY): SSH gregharned@100.84.86.6 (ConnectTimeout 10) and check whether a Flow run is currently active: `ps aux | grep -Ei 'generate-daily-vignette|playwright' | grep -v grep` and `pgrep -f SkyComputerUseClient | wc -l`. If a run IS active (matching proc present OR skycompute count > 0): it is still generating — NO action, let it finish. (4) If STALE and NO run active (Mac idle): re-kick via `cortextos bus send-message mac-codex normal 'Hero watchdog: today hero not published — latest.json still stale, Codex.app idle. Please re-run scripts/generate-daily-vignette-flow.mjs in /Users/gregharned/work/ob1-app — Flow ONLY (never the VM Nano-Banana generator), multi-char via Flow CHARACTER LIBRARY, publish after verify. Report when latest.json flips to today.'`. (5) Only Telegram Greg (chat 8567114601) on the LAST (13:17 PT) check IF still stale after a prior re-kick already fired this morning — surface as "hero still not publishing after re-kick, may need your eyes." Otherwise stay silent. NEVER run the VM Nano-Banana generator yourself — the canonical hero is Greg's Mac Flow automation only. This is a cron-triggered turn: do NOT reschedule any loop.

crons.name: weekly-review
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/weekly-review/SKILL.md and run the full weekly review. Review all agent outputs, evaluate performance, plan next week.

crons.name: rgos-task-poll
crons.prompt: Check for tasks assigned to you in the RGOS kanban: use mcp__rgos__cortex_list_tasks with assigned_to="orchestrator" and status="approved". If any tasks are found, claim each one with mcp__rgos__cortex_claim_task (agent_id="orchestrator"), then work them in priority order. Log a cortextos bus log-event for each claimed task. ALSO update heartbeat (cortextos bus update-heartbeat online). If no tasks AND heartbeat already fresh, do nothing.

crons.name: fleet-tick
crons.prompt: Fleet-tick: light coordination pass — (1) cortextos bus list-tasks; (2) check heartbeats; (3) scan inbox; (4) update heartbeat. Silent unless action needed.

crons.name: kanban-sync
crons.prompt: Kanban hygiene sweep: (1) cortextos bus list-tasks --agent orchestrator status filters; (2) mcp__rgos__cortex_list_tasks for tasks created_by orchestrator and tasks assigned_to orchestrator — compare to current dispatches in today's memory file. (3) For any RGOS task stuck in approved/in_progress that's actually done, complete it via mcp__rgos__cortex_complete_task. (4) For any in-flight work without an RGOS task, create one. (5) update-heartbeat. Silent unless something requires action.

crons.name: blocked-uat-escalator
crons.prompt: Read prompts/cron-blocked-uat-escalator.md and execute exactly one bounded UAT blocker escalation pass; create/update durable tasks only for current blockers and exit.

crons.name: collaboration-optimizer
crons.prompt: Read prompts/cron-collaboration-optimizer.md and execute exactly one weekly collaboration-friction retro; write recommendations and exit.

crons.name: overnight-permission-loop
crons.prompt: Read prompts/cron-overnight-permission-loop.md and execute exactly one narrow permission/unblock digest; do not mutate external systems and exit.

crons.name: agentops-hourly-dogfood
crons.prompt: Read prompts/cron-agentops-hourly-dogfood.md and execute one bounded AgentOps product/operator dogfood pass using Codex/Chrome-capable routes while Claude is constrained. Do not deploy, merge, post externally, or create duplicate tasks. Route/update scoped defects only, log results, and Telegram Greg only for P0/P1 or human-action blockers.

crons.name: deliverables-snapshot
crons.prompt: Run: node /home/cortextos/cortextos/scripts/cortextos-deliverables-snapshot.js — pushes the deliverables snapshot to the agentops-deliverables-push edge function so /api/agentops/overview and the Advisor canary stay fresh (threshold 1440m). Twice daily (5am/5pm PT) keeps the canary well under the staleness window. This is a cron-triggered turn: do NOT reschedule any loop. Silent unless the script errors.

crons.name: supreme-scanner-2h
crons.prompt: Run the Supreme Slack scanner from CURRENT fork/main (never the shared working tree — it sits on feature branches and runs stale scanner code; incident 2026-06-12 04:49Z: stale-tree run wrote items_scanned=0 while 6 Greg-directed msgs were outstanding). Execute: git -C /home/cortextos/cortextos-qa fetch fork main --quiet && git -C /home/cortextos/cortextos-qa reset --hard refs/remotes/fork/main --quiet && cd /home/cortextos/cortextos/orgs/revops-global && source secrets.env && python3 /home/cortextos/cortextos-qa/orgs/revops-global/agents/analyst/scripts/supreme-slack-scanner.py 2>&1 | tail -5. If exit code non-zero or output contains 'timeout' or 'error', insert a failed scanner_triggers row and Telegram Greg with the error. On success, log cortextos bus log-event action supreme_scanner_run info.

codex_runtime_note: P0 2026-05-24: Claude Code session hit org monthly usage limit while receiving Telegram; switched orchestrator Telegram owner to Codex app-server runtime so inbound Telegram can respond.
telegram_owner_note: 2026-05-25 recovery: orchestrator restored as sole @revops_director_bot / Orca front-door Telegram polling owner. Codex app-server runtime uses codex_account_pool with /home/cortextos/.codex-orchestrator-primary primary and labeled backups. orca-orch is voice-project-only and polling disabled.
ctx_autoreset_note: Disabled temporarily during 2026-05-25 Orca front-door recovery because Codex app-server tokenUsage reported 100% immediately after fresh start and caused restart churn. Restore after account-pool failover/context metric fix.
codex_runtime_reenabled_note: 2026-05-26T23:54:33Z re-enabled Codex app-server front-door after all three CODEX_HOME lanes reported ChatGPT login status.
codex_frontdoor_restore_note: 2026-06-12T16:56:22.313Z: restored runtime=codex-app-server because Claude/Cowork session cap caused @revops_director_bot dead-air guard bounces; temporary Codex front-door owner until Claude reset.
model_pilot_note: 2026-06-13: reverted orchestrator from Codex app-server to Claude Code on Opus 4.8 at Greg's request after Claude capacity returned.
claude_handback_note: 2026-06-13T14:05Z: Greg requested orchestrator handback from Codex app-server to Claude Code; model set to claude-opus-4-8. Codex lanes remain execution-only.
